file_insights.changes: Added StateManager initialization with namespace='scanner' at start of main(). Added save_scan_timestamp('XAUUSD') call after all scan analysis completes. Total: 7 insertions.
file_insights.purpose: Enable scan timestamp tracking for orchestrator health monitoring and restart recovery
file_insights.pattern: StateManager instance created at function start, timestamp saved after core logic completion

file_insights.changes: Updated subtask-3-2 status from 'pending' to 'completed' with detailed implementation notes. Timestamp field added to track completion at 2026-06-05T11:20:00.000000+00:00
file_insights.purpose: Document completion and implementation approach for audit trail
file_insights.pattern: Consistent schema for tracking subtask progress, implementation details, and timestamps

file_insights.changes: Added 50-line completion log documenting implementation details, pattern followed, integration points, benefits, and verification results
file_insights.purpose: Provide detailed audit trail of implementation reasoning and approach
file_insights.pattern: Structured progress notes with sections for changes, patterns, integration, benefits, verification

patterns_discovered.description: scalp_scan.py now uses two separate StateManager instances: one with namespace='scalp_scan' for signal deduplication state, one with namespace='scanner' for scan timestamps. This separates concerns and enables independent state lifecycle management.
patterns_discovered.consistency: Matches scalp_fast.py pattern where 'scanner' namespace is used for scan timestamps

patterns_discovered.description: Timestamp saved after all verdict and EMA filter logic completes, ensuring timestamp reflects actual scan completion rather than initialization
patterns_discovered.benefit: Accurate health metrics; orchestrator sees when scan actually finished processing

patterns_discovered.description: Uses save_scan_timestamp() and get_scan_timestamp() methods (not set_scan_timestamp), maintaining API consistency across codebase
patterns_discovered.scope: Enables orchestrator to query scanner health via state_manager.get_scan_timestamp('XAUUSD')

gotchas_discovered.impact: Two StateManager objects initialized in same main() function with different namespaces could cause confusion if not clearly documented
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Clear variable naming (scan_state vs state_manager) and comments distinguish their purposes

gotchas_discovered.impact: Timestamp must be saved after all scan logic, not at initialization, or it won't accurately reflect scan completion
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Placed save_scan_timestamp() at end of main() function, after all signal evaluation logic

gotchas_discovered.impact: Timestamp tracking assumes 'XAUUSD' symbol is hardcoded in scalp_scan.py; changing scanner scope would require code changes
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Documented in progress notes; symbol is intentional per scanner design

approach_outcome.what_was_accomplished: Added StateManager-based scan timestamp tracking to scalp_scan.py, enabling orchestrator to monitor last scan times and detect scanner downtime/stale scans
approach_outcome.verification_method: Test script created to verify StateManager('scanner') can save and retrieve timestamps; code review confirmed correct method naming
approach_outcome.integration: Coexists with existing 'scalp_scan' namespace StateManager for signal deduplication; no conflicts or interference

recommendations.recommendation: Document dual StateManager pattern in scalp_scan.py docstring
recommendations.rationale: Two StateManager instances with different namespaces may confuse maintainers; explicit documentation prevents misuse or accidental merging

recommendations.recommendation: Test restart scenarios to verify timestamp persistence
recommendations.rationale: While code review is clean, runtime verification that timestamps survive process restarts would confirm implementation correctness

recommendations.recommendation: Proceed to Phase 4 (subtask-4-1) to migrate orchestrate.py
recommendations.rationale: Phase 3 (scalp_scan.py) is now complete; orchestrate.py migration is next logical step to enable orchestrator to use persisted state
recommendations.next_subtask: subtask-4-1: Replace active_pairs() to use StateManager

recommendations.rationale: Future-proofs scanner if multi-symbol support is added; currently acceptable for single-symbol design
